data.title: Adeia Inc expected to post earnings of 33 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Adeia Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 4 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The San Jose California-based company is expected to report a 13.7% increase in revenue to $99.721 million from $87.67 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 4 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Adeia Inc is for earnings of 33 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Adeia Inc is $32.00, about 0.5% above its last closing price of $31.85

data.title: PJT Partners Chief Financial Officer Sold Shares Worth Over $1.2M
data.description:
Helen T. Meates, an officer serving as Chief Financial Officer, reported a direct sale of 8,000 Class A common shares. The shares were sold at $153.195 per share. The total sale amount reported was $1,225,560. Following the transaction, Meates directly owned 58,466 Class A common shares.

data.title: Brazil's Petrobras P-79 platform starts production ahead of schedule
data.description:
Brazil's state-controlled oil producer Petrobras said its P-79 platform, located in the Santos Basin, began production on Friday ahead of schedule.

With a capacity of 180,000 barrels of oil and gas compression of 7.2 million cubic meters per day, the unit will boost the field's installed production to approximately 1.33 million barrels of oil per day, Petrobras said in a statement.

The P-79 arrived in Brazil in February from South Korea, with operations teams on board. The platform is the eighth in operation at the Buzios field, the country's largest in reserves, which last year surpassed 1 million bpd, according to the company.

Discovered in 2010, the field is located 180 km (112 miles) off the coast of the state of Rio de Janeiro, in ultra-deep waters of the Santos Basin, at depths exceeding 2,000 meters.

data.title: Roblox Is Spending on Child Safety Features. 4 Companies That Could Benefit. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Jacob Sonenshine

Roblox is putting in place systems to protect the safety of children who use its platform, and the beneficiaries of that spending might be a group of smaller software companies.

Roblox's spending is slowing down its user growth, which disappointed the market in its most recent earnings report. But the company is under pressure to make changes following multiple lawsuits aimed at protecting children.

While Roblox fell 18% Friday, some companies that provide identity security products were seeing their stocks rise.

One example of the Roblox spending is that it will buy services from a private firm called Persona, which has an artificial intelligence-enabled age estimation technology that scans a user's face.

Another potential beneficiary is Rank One Computing Corp., which saw its stock rise 1.6% on Friday. It's a $119 million by market capitalization company that sells facial recognition and visual AI technology. Much of its business is meant to help customers identify and verify a person's face. Its nongovernment business serves financial technology companies, among others.

Its 2025 sales were $17 million, but they are expected to hit $50 million by 2027. A newly public and smaller company that isn't profitable yet, it will need financing going forward, which it could receive if investors believe in the growth story.

Elsewhere, Veritone is a $200.8 million market cap company that provides subscription-based software, and software that customers pay for every time they use it. Almost a third of its $105 million in total 2025 revenue came from identity software. Analysts expect aggressive sales growth, to $607 million by 2031, according to FactSet. Analysts forecast profitability by 2029. Its stock rose 5.9% Friday.

Two others with exposure to identity solutions are $25 billion software provider Aware and the $30 billion Experian. Aware dropped 1.7%, while Experian rose 1.4%

As for Roblox, its operating expenses are on pace to hit just over $6 billion this year, as seen by its first quarter result. The spending on child safety is just one piece of the overall company expenses, which include employees and marketing and other items.

Roblox's spending, on its own, does not create a large chunk of money for companies that sell safety products, but its situation illustrates that more companies could start spending in this way.

Write to Jacob Sonenshine at jacob.sonenshine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Leading Independent Proxy Advisory Firm Gives Full Endorsement to Sylogist-Endorsed Nominees, Rejects OneMove's Case
data.description:
Key points:
  • ISS recommends shareholders vote FOR Sylogist's six director nominees and Board-supported OneMove nominee Mary Filippelli using only the BLUE proxy
  • ISS recommends shareholders WITHHOLD from OneMove's three remaining nominees: Jonny Franklin-Adams, Rhonda Bassett-Spiers, and Tyler Proud
  • ISS recommends shareholders vote FOR ratification of the Shareholder Rights Plan
  • Shareholders who have questions or require assistance voting should contact Laurel Hill Advisory Group Toll-Free by calling 1-877-452-7184 (Canada & USA) or 1-416-304-0211 (international), texting "INFO" to either number, or e-mailing assistance@laurelhill.com
  • For more information and voting instructions, visit www.sylogist.com/agm-sm

Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - May 1, 2026) - Sylogist Ltd. ("Sylogist" or the "Corporation"), a leading public sector SaaS company, is pleased to announce that leading independent proxy advisory firm Institutional Shareholder Services Inc. ("ISS") has recommended that shareholders vote FOR the Corporation's six director nominees and Board-supported OneMove nominee Mary Filippelli, using only the BLUE proxy, at the Annual and Special Meeting of Shareholders (the "Meeting") to be held on May 12, 2026. Shareholders are urged to vote well in advance of the proxy voting deadline of May 8, 2026 at 10 AM MT.

The Management Information Circular and BLUE form of proxy are available at www.sylogist.com/agm-sm and under the Corporation's profile on SEDAR+.

Independent Proxy Advisory Firm Recommendation

In its report to shareholders, ISS stated that OneMove "has not established a compelling case at this time for additional change, much less a majority position."

ISS made its recommendations following engagement with both Sylogist and Mr. Proud. It has assessed the facts, and it has reached an informed and independent conclusion. These provide clear, independent validation for the Board's approach of having repeatedly sought constructive engagement and reasonable settlement with Mr. Proud. ISS also noted that the Board has listened to shareholder feedback and taken appropriate actions to drive the business forward despite the chaos. ISS also made clear that it is Mr. Proud who has repeatedly chosen conflict over the Corporation's best interests.

ISS specifically recognized:

  • The Board has responded appropriately to shareholder concerns, through leadership change, governance refreshment, and its decision to support dissident nominee Mary Filippelli

  • That "[Mr.] Proud advanced a broad and evolving set of demands," "the board substantively addressed several of these points during negotiations," and that an early miscalculation of shareholder support may have driven his decision to escalate rather than settle

  • OneMove's slate and CEO claims provide "little assurance that the dissident has thoughtfully considered board composition and raises questions regarding whether the dissident's intended CEO has capacity to work with a board that does not remain aligned with the dissident agenda"

  • That it found "no reason to conclude that the ongoing CEO search will not be conducted in accordance with the best interests of shareholders"

ISS also recommended shareholders vote FOR ratification of the Shareholder Rights Plan, noting that such plans "ensure that shareholders are treated equally in a control transaction by precluding creeping acquisitions or the acquisition of a control block through private agreements between a few large shareholders."

Glass Lewis, another proxy advisory firm, also issued a report. Although Glass Lewis declined to engage with the Corporation and its report contains certain inaccuracies, its core conclusion concurred with ISS that Tyler Proud and OneMove have not successfully made the case for majority change. Glass Lewis did support the majority of Sylogist recommendations, but recommended support for one of OneMove's nominees and a withhold vote on one of the incumbents.

A Fair and Proportionate Compromise

The Board's recommendation reflects a fair and proportionate outcome: proportional OneMove representation on the Board, consistent with Mr. Proud's ownership interest. This position is supported by ISS and many of the Corporation's largest shareholders. Mr. Proud has refused to accept an outcome that is in the interests of all shareholders, instead demanding majority control of four of seven Board seats - nearly four times his ownership interest - without paying shareholders a premium for that control.

Board Recommendation

The Board of Directors unanimously recommends that shareholders vote using only the BLUE proxy or BLUE voting instruction form as follows:

ResolutionBoard Recommendation
Receipt of financial statements for year ended December 31, 2025✓ FOR
Appointment of KPMG LLP as auditors✓ FOR
Election of Sylogist's six director nominees + Mary Filippelli✓ FOR
Remaining three OneMove nominees (Franklin-Adams, Bassett-Spiers, Proud)x WITHHOLD
Ratification of Shareholder Rights Plan✓ FOR

How to Vote

Shareholders are urged to vote well in advance of the proxy voting deadline of May 8, 2026 at 10 AM MT. Votes may be cast online at www.proxyvote.com or www.investorvote.com, by telephone at 1-866-732-VOTE (8683) toll free in North America, or by completing and returning the BLUE form of proxy in the prepaid envelope provided.

Shareholder Questions or Require Voting Assistance?

If you have questions or require assistance voting, please contact Sylogist's proxy solicitation agent:

Laurel Hill Advisory Group Toll-Free (Canada & U.S.): 1-877-452-7184 | International: 1-416-304-0211 Text "INFO" to either number | Email: assistance@laurelhill.com

About Sylogist

Sylogist provides mission-critical SaaS solutions to public sector customers globally across the government, non-profit, and education market segments. The Corporation's stock is traded on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol SYZ. Information about Sylogist can be found at www.sedarplus.ca (http://www.sedarplus.ca) or at www.sylogist.com (http://www.sylogist.com).

Media Contact:

FGS Longview

Joel Shaffer, Justine Hall

sylogist@fgslongview.com

Disclaimer

Forward-looking Statements

Certain statements in this news release may be forward-looking statements within the meaning of applicable securities laws and regulations. These statements typically use words such as will, believe, intend, ensure, would or continue, seek or the negative of these terms, variations thereof or similar terminology. By their very nature, forward-looking statements are based on assumptions and involve inherent risks and uncertainties, both general and specific in nature. It is therefore possible that the beliefs and plans and other forward-looking expectations expressed herein will not be achieved or will prove inaccurate. Forward-looking statements in this news release include those relating to the potential for continued engagement with OneMove, the intentions of OneMove and the impact of electing its nominees, the outcome of the votes at the Meeting, the potential requirement for the Corporation to pay OneMove's expenses at the meeting, the approval of the Rights Plan by shareholders and its effect. Although Sylogist believes that the expectations reflected in these forward-looking statements are reasonable, it provides no assurance that these expectations will prove to have been correct. Forward-looking information involves risks, uncertainties and other factors that could cause actual events, results, performance, prospects and opportunities to differ materially from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking information. Such risks and uncertainties include the possibility of further engagement with OneMove, that the outcome of the votes at the Meeting will have the anticipated impact, the amount and nature of OneMove's expenses, which could be material to the Corporation, the nature and extent of OneMove's intentions and their potential adverse or other impact on the business, operations and financial condition of the Corporation, and that the impact of the ratification of the plan by shareholders or the failure to obtain such ratification. Additional information regarding some of these risks, uncertainties and other factors may be found in the Corporation's Annual Information Form for year ended December 31, 2025, and in the Management's Discussion and Analysis for the year and quarter ended December 31, 2025, and other documents available on the Corporation's profile at www.sedarplus.ca. Although, Sylogist believes that the material assumptions and factors used in preparing the forward-looking information in this news release are reasonable, undue reliance should not be placed on such information, which only applies as of the date of this news release, and no assurance can be given that such events will occur. Sylogist disclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise any forward-looking information, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, other than as required by law.

data.title: Pershing Square Secures $350 Million Senior Credit Facilities to Support PSUS Anchor Investment
data.description:
Pershing Square entered multiple material agreements tied to its IPO and financing plans. The company secured a $350 million senior secured credit package, including a $250 million revolving facility and a $100 million term loan led by Bank of America, maturing April 30, 2029, to fund its anchor investment in Pershing Square USA and bolster liquidity. In addition, Pershing Square finalized two registration rights agreements to facilitate potential future resales by current and future holders, consistent with its prospectus disclosures. Together, these steps are intended to support capital access and post-IPO trading flexibility.

Agreement 1: Pershing Square Secures $350 Million Senior Credit Facilities Led by Bank of America

  • Agreement type: Senior secured revolving credit facility and term loan
  • Counterparty: Bank of America and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 30 2026 / Apr 30 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 3 years, matures Apr 30 2029
  • Reason: Fund PSUS anchor investment and enhance liquidity

Agreement 2: Pershing Square Finalizes Registration Rights Agreement With Investors Tied to IPO

  • Agreement type: Registration rights agreement
  • Counterparty: Various investors
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 28 2026 / Apr 28 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Facilitate potential resales by shareholders post-IPO

Agreement 3: Pershing Square Executes Additional Registration Rights Agreement for Future Holder Resales

  • Agreement type: Registration rights agreement
  • Counterparty: Various investors
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 28 2026 / Apr 28 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Provide orderly liquidity options for future holders

Original SEC Filing: PERSHING SQUARE INC. [ PS ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Herbalife Completes $1.45 Billion Refinancing With New Notes and Credit Facilities
data.description:
Herbalife completed a comprehensive refinancing on April 29, 2026, issuing $800 million of 7.750% senior secured notes due 2033 and closing $650 million of new senior secured credit facilities. Proceeds, together with cash, were used to fully redeem its 12.250% senior secured notes due 2029 for approximately $852.8 million, including premium and interest. The transactions lower borrowing costs, extend maturities and bolster liquidity under a covenant package aligned with the company's capital structure.

Agreement 1: Herbalife Prices $800 Million 7.75% Senior Secured Notes Due 2033 to Refinance Debt

  • Agreement type: 7.750% Senior Secured Notes due 2033 (Indenture)
  • Counterparty: Citibank, as trustee and collateral agent; initial purchasers
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 29 2026 / Apr 29 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through May 1 2033
  • Reason: Refinance 2029 secured notes and extend maturities

Agreement 2: Herbalife Closes $650 Million Senior Secured Credit Facilities Led by Rabobank

  • Agreement type: Senior Secured Credit Facilities (Term A and Revolving) via Ninth Amendment
  • Counterparty: Rabobank, as administrative agent; Citizens Bank, as collateral agent; lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 29 2026 / Apr 29 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Apr 29 2031 (with springing maturities)
  • Reason: Refinance existing facilities and enhance liquidity

Agreement 3: Herbalife Redeems 12.25% 2029 Secured Notes for About $853 Million Following Refinancing

  • Agreement terminated: 12.250% Senior Secured Notes due 2029 (Indenture)
  • Counterparty: Citibank, as trustee and collateral agent
  • Original agreement date: Apr 12 2024
  • Termination date: Apr 29 2026
  • Termination type: Early
  • Exit fees / payments: Approx. $852.8 million (including premium and interest)
  • Reason: Redeemed with proceeds from new notes and credit facilities

Original SEC Filing: HERBALIFE LTD. [ HLF ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Farm Bill Finally Made Progress in Congress. Here Are 3 Key Issues to Watch. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Evie Liu

The long-delayed Farm Bill has finally begun moving again in Washington. On Thursday, the House passed its version, but the legislation still faces a difficult path in the Senate, where narrow margins and different priorities could reshape it.

Congress has been trying for years to pass a new Farm Bill — a battleground where fiscal policy, environmental concerns, and industrial interests collide — relying on temporary extensions since the 2018 law expired in 2023. Budget constraints have sharpened the fight, since expanding support for one group often requires cuts elsewhere.

In the coming weeks, investors should watch:

  • Whether the bill further trims SNAP funding or reverses previous cuts, which could impact lower-income consumption, food producers, and discount retailers;
  • Whether the bill approves stronger farm subsidies, which could benefit crop insurers as well as suppliers of agricultural input and equipment;
  • Whether the Congress supports easier access to high-ethanol biofuels, which would favor corn growers and ethanol producers, but make things more costly for meatpackers and oil refiners.

The Senate will ultimately decide how these tensions are resolved.

Food Aid

Why it matters: Spending on SNAP, the food aid program, accounts for the bulk of Farm Bill outlays and is the biggest and most visible divide.

What's the debate: Republicans frame SNAP reform as a way to control deficits and encourage work. Fiscal conservatives have pushed to limit spending growth, tighten eligibility rules, and in some cases shift more administrative costs to states.

Democrats, on the other hand, say that the program is an efficient form of economic support during periods of high food inflation, and that recent benefit reductions have already strained low-income households and should be reversed or expanded.

The House-passed farm bill doesn't expand SNAP and maintains cuts enacted in last year's tax-and-spending law.

Investment implications: Reduced SNAP funding could hurt grocery sales as low-income households cut spending. This could impact packaged food companies like General Mills and Campbell, as well as retailers like Walmart, Kroger, and Dollar Tree.

Farm Safety Net

Why it matters: Farm income has fallen sharply from the 2022 peak as crop producers face pressure from volatile crop prices and high input costs.

What's the debate: Farm groups have long pushed for stronger income protection such as crop insurance subsidies and disaster assistance aid.

Republicans from farm states and many Democrats representing rural districts support those changes, making this one of the few areas with bipartisan backing.

The House bill includes changes to the crop insurance program, including increased premium subsidies for veteran farmers and ranchers.

Farm groups have broadly welcomed the bill as a step toward greater stability for producers.

Still, budget watchdogs argue that subsidies are too generous and the system disproportionately benefits large farms, which entrenches consolidation in agriculture.

Investment implications: A stronger safety net can benefit crop insurers and indirectly support agricultural input suppliers, including fertilizer producers like Nutrien, CF Industries, and Mosaic, seed and crop protection companies like Corteva and Bayer, and farm equipment manufacturers like Deere & Company and AGCO.

Ethanol Blend in Biofuels

Why it matters: Biofuel policy directly affects commodity demand and gasoline markets, making it a proxy fight between agriculture and energy interests.

What's the debate: Ethanol is primarily made from corn. Farm groups have been pushing the government to remove regulatory and infrastructure limits that currently restrict consumer access to E15 — gasoline with 15% ethanol blend.

E15 is typically restricted in summer months due to emissions rules, though regulators have repeatedly granted waivers to allow its sale. The farm industry is asking for permission for year-round E15 sales nationwide.

Corn-state lawmakers — mostly Republicans but with notable Democratic support — are supportive. Oil refiners have historically opposed broader ethanol access because it could raise their compliance and blending costs.

The House-passed farm bill didn't include language for year-round E15 access. The House is expected to vote on a stand-alone E15 bill on May 13, according to Reuters.

Investment implications: Higher ethanol demand could support corn prices, benefiting crop processors like Archer Daniels Midland and companies tied to the biofuel supply chain, including ethanol producers such as Green Plains and REX American Resources.

By contrast, oil refiners could see their margins cut due to higher operational and compliance costs associated with the changes. Higher corn prices could also raise feed costs and livestock prices, squeezing margins at meat processing companies like Tyson Foods and Pilgrim's Pride.

Write to Evie Liu at evie.liu@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Cineverse to Exchange Series A Preferred for Up to 1.5M Common Shares With OCI-Cinedigm
data.description:
Cineverse entered into an Exchange Agreement with OCI-Cinedigm to convert 3.118 shares of Series A Preferred into up to 1.5 million shares of Class A common stock. The exchange will occur in five equal tranches starting May 1, 2026, with shares per tranche determined by the 5-day VWAP ending the trading day before each exchange. Preferred shares exchanged in each tranche will be retired and restored to authorized but unissued status. The move is intended to streamline the capital structure.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Exchange agreement for preferred stock into common stock
  • Counterparty: OCI-Cinedigm
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 27 2026 / May 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Five equal tranches beginning May 01 2026
  • Reason: Reduce preferred obligations and simplify capital structure

Original SEC Filing: Cineverse Corp. [ CNVS ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Dave Inc expected to post earnings of $2.87 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Dave Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Los Angeles California-based company is expected to report a 40.4% increase in revenue to $151.617 million from $108 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 9 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Dave Inc is for earnings of $2.87 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 5.1% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Dave Inc is $326.00, about 19.9% above its last closing price of $271.99
